From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Czech and Slovak word for a municipality

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b7/Libi%C5%A1%2C_M%C4%9Blnick%C3%A1_2%2C_z_Neratovic_z_Ml%C3%A1de%C5%BEnick%C3%A9%2C_tabule_hranice_obc%C3%AD.jpg/330px-Libi%C5%A1%2C_M%C4%9Blnick%C3%A1_2%2C_z_Neratovic_z_Ml%C3%A1de%C5%BEnick%C3%A9%2C_tabule_hranice_obc%C3%AD.jpg)](/wiki/File:Libi%C5%A1,_M%C4%9Blnick%C3%A1_2,_z_Neratovic_z_Ml%C3%A1de%C5%BEnick%C3%A9,_tabule_hranice_obc%C3%AD.jpg)

Signs showing the border of two municipalities

***Obec*** (Czech: [[ˈobɛts]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Czech "Help:IPA/Czech"), Slovak: [[ˈɔbets]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Slovak "Help:IPA/Slovak"); plural *obce*) is the [Czech](/wiki/Czech_language "Czech language") and [Slovak](/wiki/Slovak_language "Slovak language") word for a [municipality](/wiki/Municipality "Municipality") (in the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"), in [Slovakia](/wiki/Slovakia "Slovakia") and abroad). The literal meaning of the word is "[commune](/wiki/Intentional_community "Intentional community")" or "[community](/wiki/Community "Community")". It is the smallest administrative unit that is governed by elected representatives. Cities and towns are also municipalities.

## Definition

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Obec&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Definition")]

The legal definition (according to the Czech [code of law](/wiki/Code_of_law "Code of law")[[1]](#cite_note-czlaw-1) with similar definition in the Slovak code of law[[2]](#cite_note-sklaw-2)) is: *"The municipality is a basic territorial self-governing community of citizens; it forms a territorial unit, which is defined by the boundary of the municipality."*

Every municipality is composed of one or more [cadastral](/wiki/Cadastre "Cadastre") areas. Every municipality is also composed of one or more municipal parts ([Czech](/wiki/Czech_language "Czech language"): *části obce*), which are usually town quarters or villages. A municipality can have its own flag and coat of arms.[[1]](#cite_note-czlaw-1)[[2]](#cite_note-sklaw-2)

## Czech Republic

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Obec&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Czech Republic")]

See also: [List of cities and towns in the Czech Republic](/wiki/List_of_cities_and_towns_in_the_Czech_Republic "List of cities and towns in the Czech Republic")

Almost the entire area of the Czech Republic is divided into municipalities, with the only exception being [military training areas](/wiki/Unincorporated_Areas_of_the_Czech_Republic "Unincorporated Areas of the Czech Republic"). The smaller municipalities consist only of one village. A municipality usually has the same name as its most populated settlement, which usually contains the municipal office. However there are several exceptions, for example municipalities created by mergers of formerly separate municipalities (such as [Brandýs nad Labem-Stará Boleslav](/wiki/Brand%C3%BDs_nad_Labem-Star%C3%A1_Boleslav "Brandýs nad Labem-Stará Boleslav") or [Orlické Podhůří](/wiki/Orlick%C3%A9_Podh%C5%AF%C5%99%C3%AD "Orlické Podhůří")).

A municipality can obtain the title of a [city](/wiki/Statutory_city_(Czech_Republic) "Statutory city (Czech Republic)") (*statutární město*), [town](/wiki/Town "Town") (*město*) or [market town](/wiki/M%C4%9Bstys "Městys") (*městys*). While all of these are municipalities from the point of view of the law, they are usually referred to by their titles and not as municipalities. Municipalities without any other status are just called municipalities. Statutory cities can have self-governing subdivisions, so-called city parts or city districts (*městská část*), which are somewhat similar to municipalities in that they have their own town halls and local government.[[1]](#cite_note-czlaw-1) Town and market town are above all ceremonial labels bestowed on municipalities with notable population, history and regional significance.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

A special type of municipality is the capital [Prague](/wiki/Prague "Prague"), which has simultaneously the status of a municipality and a [region](/wiki/Regions_of_the_Czech_Republic "Regions of the Czech Republic"), and which is treated by special law.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The law makes it possible for municipalities which were stripped of town status during the Communist period to retain that status automatically. For any other municipality to gain town status, it must have population over 3,000 and must pass an assessment by the chairman of the parliament. For market town status, population is not a condition.[[1]](#cite_note-czlaw-1) The newest Czech town is [Chýně](/wiki/Ch%C3%BDn%C4%9B "Chýně"), which obtained the status in 2023.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Some municipalities have [extended competencies](/wiki/Districts_of_the_Czech_Republic#Municipalities_with_extended_competence "Districts of the Czech Republic") of [delegated state administration](/wiki/Districts_of_the_Czech_Republic#Municipalities_with_commissioned_local_authority "Districts of the Czech Republic") for the territory of the municipality and for surrounding municipalities.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### Statistics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Obec&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Statistics")]

Count of municipalities by category of population (as of January 2025)[[7]](#cite_note-pop-7)

|  | Total | Cities | Towns | Market towns | Other municipalities |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Over 99,999 | **6** | 6 | – | – | – |
| 50,000–99,999 | **12** | 12 | – | – | – |
| 25,000–49,999 | **21** | 9 | 12 | – | – |
| 10,000–24,999 | **93** | – | 93 | – | – |
| 5,000–9,999 | **145** | – | 141 | – | 4 |
| 2,500–4,999 | **284** | – | 179 | 9 | 96 |
| 1,000–2,499 | **974** | – | 128 | 112 | 734 |
| 500–999 | **1,378** | – | 17 | 88 | 1,273 |
| Under 500 | **3,341** | – | 13 | 23 | 3,305 |
| Total | **6,254** | **27** | **583** | **232** | **5,412** |

Population in municipalities by status (as of January 2025)[[7]](#cite_note-pop-7)

|  | Total | Cities | Towns | Market towns | Other municipalities |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Average | **1,744** | 138,934 | 6,403 | 1,190 | 582 |
| Median | **453** | 63,474 | 4,156 | 1,049 | 385 |
| Minimum | **16** | 33,852 ([Třinec](/wiki/T%C5%99inec "Třinec")) | 70 ([Přebuz](/wiki/P%C5%99ebuz "Přebuz")) | 159 ([Levín](/wiki/Lev%C3%ADn "Levín")) | 16 ([Vysoká Lhota](/wiki/Vysok%C3%A1_Lhota "Vysoká Lhota")) |
| Maximum | **1,397,880** | 1,397,880 ([Prague](/wiki/Prague "Prague")) | 36,815 ([Česká Lípa](/wiki/%C4%8Cesk%C3%A1_L%C3%ADpa "Česká Lípa")) | 4,404 ([Nehvizdy](/wiki/Nehvizdy "Nehvizdy")) | 5,496 ([Horoměřice](/wiki/Horom%C4%9B%C5%99ice "Horoměřice")) |
| Total | **10,909,500** | **3,751,223** | **3,732,986** | **275,998** | **3,149,293** |

The smallest municipalities by area are [Závist](/wiki/Z%C3%A1vist_(Blansko_District) "Závist (Blansko District)") (0.42 km2) and [Strukov](/wiki/Strukov "Strukov") (0.53 km2).[[8]](#cite_note-8) The biggest are the cities of [Prague](/wiki/Prague "Prague") (496.21 km2), [Brno](/wiki/Brno "Brno") (230.18 km2) and [Ostrava](/wiki/Ostrava "Ostrava") (214.23 km2), and the town of [Ralsko](/wiki/Ralsko "Ralsko") (170.23 km2, including a former military area, with only about 2,000 inhabitants).[[9]](#cite_note-9)

### List of municipalities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Obec&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: List of municipalities")]

Main article: [List of municipalities in the Czech Republic](/wiki/List_of_municipalities_in_the_Czech_Republic "List of municipalities in the Czech Republic")

## Slovakia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Obec&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Slovakia")]

See also: [List of municipalities in Slovakia](/wiki/List_of_municipalities_in_Slovakia "List of municipalities in Slovakia")

As of 2020, there are [2,890 municipalities](/wiki/List_of_municipalities_and_towns_in_Slovakia "List of municipalities and towns in Slovakia") in Slovakia, with 141 of them [being a city or a town](/wiki/List_of_cities_and_towns_in_Slovakia "List of cities and towns in Slovakia").[[10]](#cite_note-10)

After meeting certain conditions such as population over 5,000, being well accessible, having cultural or economical significance and having an urban style of settlement, a municipality can be declared a town (*mesto*).[[2]](#cite_note-sklaw-2)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Obec&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: See also")]
